mnemonics.title: Waterfall Phases
mnemonics.content: READ IT: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Maintenance (each phase completes before next)

mnemonics.title: Agile Manifesto
mnemonics.content: I WOC: Individuals & Interactions > Processes, Working Software > Docs, Customer Collaboration > Contracts, Responding to Change > Plans

mnemonics.title: CMMI Levels
mnemonics.content: IDDQO: Initial → Defined → Quantitatively Managed → Optimizing (skip Managed in sequence)

mnemonics.title: Spiral Model Steps
mnemonics.content: PRER: Planning → Risk analysis → Engineering → Evaluation (repeats in cycles)

mnemonics.title: Scrum Roles
mnemonics.content: PST: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Team (Development Team)

mnemonics.title: Verification vs Verification
mnemonics.content: V-V: Verification = Right (building the right product), Validation = Correctly (building the product correctly)

mnemonics.title: Process Activities
mnemonics.content: SDEM: Specification → Design → Evolution → Maintenance (or Design, Implementation, Validation, Evolution)

acronyms.usage: The overall process of developing software from requirements to maintenance
Framework for improving software development processes
Fast development using prototypes and component assembly
Agile methodology emphasizing continuous feedback and quality
Standard language for software design visualization
Tools to automate software development processes

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a literal waterfall - steps going down sequentially, no going back up. Like stairs descending.
Think of the letter V - development phases on left slope going down, corresponding testing phases on right slope going up, meeting at integration point.
Draw as a spiral starting from center, expanding outward in cycles - each loop has 4 quadrants representing planning, risk, engineering, evaluation.
Show as circular arrows repeating - each cycle produces working software increment, getting closer to final product each time.
Pyramid with 5 levels - base is Initial (chaotic), top is Optimizing (continuous improvement).

analogies.analogy: Like building a house: first architect draws plans, then workers build foundation, then frame, then walls - each step complete before next.
Like an architect's sketch before building a house - a preliminary version to get feedback and refine ideas before full construction.
Like writing and revising an essay repeatedly - each draft improves upon the previous, identifying problems (risks) early and fixing them.
Like a sprint in athletics - short, intense effort (2-4 weeks) to complete a specific goal, with daily check-ins and a final review.
Like building with LEGO blocks - adding one functional piece at a time until the complete structure emerges.
